title: Rich Gooey Japanese Cured Salmon Bowl
description: A quick, Whole30-friendly bowl featuring silky cured salmon, creamy cauliflower rice, and a crunchy cauliflowercrispy topping.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillet
ingredients.notes: Cured with sea salt and erythritol

instructions: 1. Rub the salmon fillet with 1 tsp of pink Himalayan salt and 1/2 tsp of erythritol, then let it cure in the fridge for 30 minutes.
2. Heat 1 tsp of sesame oil in a skillet over medium heat and sear the salmon until the skin is crisp and the flesh is cooked through, about 4–5 minutes per side.
3. While the salmon cooks, steam 1 cup of cauliflower rice until tender, about 5 minutes.
4. Spread the cauliflower rice on a shallow plate and drizzle 1 tsp of sesame oil over it.
5. In the same skillet, add the cauliflower crisp and toast until golden and crunchy, about 3 minutes; set aside.
6. Flake the cured salmon into bite-sized pieces and gently fold into the cauliflower rice.
7. Drizzle 1 tbsp of low-sodium tamari over the bowl, then sprinkle toasted sesame seeds, microgreens, and shredded nori flakes on top.
8. Serve immediately, optionally adding a squeeze of lime for brightness.
